items.title: Hong Kong regulator orders new anti-phishing measures for crypto platforms
items.summary: Hong Kong’s regulator has ordered crypto platforms and online brokers to meet newly issued phishing-resistant login requirements within the next 12 months.

items.title: GPT-5.5-Cyber built a zlib fuzzing lab in a day
items.summary: We’re running Patch the Planet, an ongoing collaboration with OpenAI that pairs Trail of Bits engineers directly with more than 30 open-source projects. Its goal is to front-run a serious problem facing open-source maintainers: highly capable models like GPT-5.5-Cyber will soon c

items.title: Billions flowing out of bitcoin ETFs and private credit funds suggest rising market risks
items.summary: Redemption requests in the $2 trillion private credit market surged to $15.6 billion in the second quarter, dwarfing bitcoin ETF outflows.

items.title: Crypto remains resilient in face of renewed Middle East tensions
items.summary: BTC rose 1.2% to $63,000 and Nasdaq futures jumped 2.6% with markets seemingly unconcerned by U.S. airstrikes on Iran. Bitcoin is now up 9% since the end of June.

items.title: Mutation testing comes to DAML
items.summary: In April we released Mewt, our open-source mutation-testing engine that finds the gaps in your test suite. Today we’re expanding it with support for DAML, the language Canton Network applications are written in. Mewt now reads DAML, generates several classes of mutants (including

items.title: Polymarket seeks approval to bring margin trading to U.S. customers
items.summary: Polymarket's application to allow users to take positions that are not fully collateralized follows authorization granted to rival Kalshi in March.

items.title: Bitcoin's $60,000-$70,000 range becomes third longest consolidation in history
items.summary: Bitcoin has spent 307 days in the $60,000-$70,000 range, the third longest consolidation in any $10,000 price band.

items.title: Metaplanet explores bringing bitcoin-backed digital credit to Japan
items.summary: The bitcoin treasury company is working with JPYC and Progmat to explore tokenized credit products backed by bitcoin, aiming to create efficient, 24/7 credit markets in Japan.

items.title: Bitcoin gets a green light from a reliable momentum gauge. Here are key levels to watch
items.summary: A smoother long-term MACD just flipped bullish, pointing to more upside. Here are key levels that will decide if the ongoing bounce turns into a full blown uptrend.

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin tops $64,000 as selling pressure on Coinbase eases
items.summary: Spot bitcoin funds lost about $95 million on Thursday and ether funds roughly $52 million, ending the one bright spot in crypto's institutional flows even as prices rallied.

items.title: XRP up 2% as buyers push through $1.10 resistance
items.summary: A late-session volume surge carried XRP above its recent range, with traders now watching whether $1.10 can hold as support.

items.title: Bitcoin's quiet split: Strong in USD, lagging in JPY as Yen rises on intervention fears
items.summary: A sharp rise in the yen has left bitcoin and other major cryptocurrencies underperforming in yen terms compared with their dollar-based trading pairs.

items.title: Bitcoin zips higher to nearly $64,000 as chip rally and yen strength drive gains
items.summary: Bitcoin is up 4.2% over seven days that contained an oil shock, a bond selloff and two rounds of U.S. strikes on Iran.

items.title: TeraWulf eyes $3.5B debt raise for Anthropic-linked data center: Report
items.summary: Bitcoin miner TeraWulf is reportedly seeking $3.5 billion in debt financing led by Morgan Stanley for its Kentucky data center campus leased by AI company Anthropic.

items.title: Metaplanet explores Bitcoin-backed digital credit with JPYC in Japan
items.summary: Metaplanet said it is studying Bitcoin-backed digital credit products in Japan with JPYC and Progmat, but no product has been launched.

items.title: Bitcoin returns to $64.3K with new three-week BTC price highs imminent
items.summary: Bitcoin bulls faced "crucial resistance" at $65,000 next as BTC price action diverged from oil and US dollar strength.

items.title: EU parliament passes ‘chat control,’ allowing private chat scans until 2028
items.summary: EU lawmakers have again allowed tech firms to scan chats for abuse material, but exempted end-to-end encrypted messages.

items.title: Robinhood Chain sees over $70M in ETH bridged during first week
items.summary: Robinhood’s choice to use Ethereum further solidifies its position as the “ultimate settlement layer and liquidity foundation for tokenized assets,” said HashKey Group’s Tim Sun.

items.title: Zcash targets July 28 launch for Ironwood network upgrade
items.summary: Zcash’s next upgrade will replace the once-compromised Orchard pool and may offer clues about whether counterfeit tokens were created via a recent bug.

items.title: Hackers tried to backdoor Injective npm package to steal wallet keys
items.summary: The incident is significant for developers and applications that handle Injective wallet workflows, Socket researchers said.
